predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

drought.explanation: Drought risk is 48.5% (medium) because expected precipitation is 0.0 mm/24h, observed mean temperature over the last 24 hours is 13.4 C, and soil moisture is 0.0% of the wet reference. For drought, precipitation below 5.0 mm/24h and temperatures above 30.0 C increase risk. The score now uses previous years for the same seasonal window, so one dry forecast day is not enough by itself to create high drought risk. Current temperature is 13.4 C. In this calculation: precipitation strongly increases risk, temperature reduces risk, dry soil strongly increases risk, 30-day historical dryness reduces risk.

normal_limits.high: near-zero precipitation, dry soil, and temperature above 30.0 C
normal_limits.low: above 5.0 mm/24h or sufficiently moist soil
normal_limits.medium: very low precipitation and temperature rising toward 30.0 C

drought.title: Drought

flood.explanation: Flood risk is 0.0% (low) because the forecast shows 0.0 mm of precipitation in the next 24 hours, then adjusts that signal using recent wetness versus same-season historical rainfall and terrain. The high-risk reference threshold is 50.0 mm/24h; values below 20.0 mm/24h are normally treated as low risk.

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.2 C. The forecast maximum for the next 24 hours is 13.6 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.high: near/above 38.0 C
normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 26.0% (low) because maximum forecast gusts are 10.0 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

storm.title: Storm / strong wind

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 13.1% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.2 C, and wind gusts 10.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score. Current temperature is 13.4 C.

normal_limits.high: temperature above 32.0 C, wind above 12.0 m/s, and dry soil
normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

wildfire.title: Wildfire

drought.explanation: Drought risk is 35.1% (low) because expected precipitation is 0.3 mm/24h, observed mean temperature over the last 24 hours is 13.2 C, and soil moisture is unknown. For drought, precipitation below 5.0 mm/24h and temperatures above 30.0 C increase risk. The score now uses previous years for the same seasonal window, so one dry forecast day is not enough by itself to create high drought risk. In this calculation: precipitation strongly increases risk, temperature reduces risk, 30-day historical dryness reduces risk.

normal_limits.high: near-zero precipitation, dry soil, and temperature above 30.0 C
normal_limits.low: above 5.0 mm/24h or sufficiently moist soil
normal_limits.medium: very low precipitation and temperature rising toward 30.0 C

drought.title: Drought

flood.explanation: Flood risk is 0.4% (low) because the forecast shows 0.3 mm of precipitation in the next 24 hours, then adjusts that signal using recent wetness versus same-season historical rainfall and terrain. The high-risk reference threshold is 50.0 mm/24h; values below 20.0 mm/24h are normally treated as low risk.

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 13.6 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 21.1% (low) because maximum forecast gusts are 8.1 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

storm.title: Storm / strong wind

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 6.9% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 13.6 C, and wind gusts 8.1 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

normal_limits.high: temperature above 32.0 C, wind above 12.0 m/s, and dry soil
normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 13.1% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.2 C, and wind gusts 10.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score. Current temperature is 13.4 C.

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 8.8% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.1 C, and wind gusts 11.9 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.8 C, and wind gusts 14.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

normal_limits.high: temperature above 32.0 C, wind above 12.0 m/s, and dry soil
normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

wildfire.title: Wildfire

daily.summary: 2026-04-27: highest forecast indices are storm 36.4 (low), drought 35.7 (low), wildfire 8.9 (low).

drought.explanation: Drought risk is 30.0% (low) because expected precipitation is 2.0 mm/24h, observed mean temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.8 C, and soil moisture is unknown. For drought, precipitation below 5.0 mm/24h and temperatures above 30.0 C increase risk. The score now uses previous years for the same seasonal window, so one dry forecast day is not enough by itself to create high drought risk. In this calculation: precipitation moderately increases risk, temperature reduces risk, 30-day historical dryness reduces risk.

normal_limits.high: near-zero precipitation, dry soil, and temperature above 30.0 C
normal_limits.low: above 5.0 mm/24h or sufficiently moist soil
normal_limits.medium: very low precipitation and temperature rising toward 30.0 C

drought.title: Drought

flood.explanation: Flood risk is 2.6% (low) because the forecast shows 2.0 mm of precipitation in the next 24 hours, then adjusts that signal using recent wetness versus same-season historical rainfall and terrain. The high-risk reference threshold is 50.0 mm/24h; values below 20.0 mm/24h are normally treated as low risk.

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.9 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.high: near/above 38.0 C
normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 33.8% (low) because maximum forecast gusts are 13.0 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

The high-risk reference threshold is 50.0 mm/24h; values below 20.0 mm/24h are normally treated as low risk.

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.4 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.high: near/above 38.0 C
normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 45.2% (medium) because maximum forecast gusts are 17.4 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

storm.title: Storm / strong wind

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 8.9% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.4 C, and wind gusts 17.4 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

normal_limits.high: temperature above 32.0 C, wind above 12.0 m/s, and dry soil
normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

wildfire.title: Wildfire

daily.summary: 2026-04-29: highest forecast indices are storm 45.2 (medium), drought 30.6 (low), wildfire 8.9 (low).

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

drought.explanation: Drought risk is 48.5% (medium) because expected precipitation is 0.0 mm/24h, observed mean temperature over the last 24 hours is 13.4 C, and soil moisture is 0.0% of the wet reference. For drought, precipitation below 5.0 mm/24h and temperatures above 30.0 C increase risk. The score now uses previous years for the same seasonal window, so one dry forecast day is not enough by itself to create high drought risk. Current temperature is 13.4 C. In this calculation: precipitation strongly increases risk, temperature reduces risk, dry soil strongly increases risk, 30-day historical dryness reduces risk.

normal_limits.high: near-zero precipitation, dry soil, and temperature above 30.0 C
normal_limits.low: above 5.0 mm/24h or sufficiently moist soil
normal_limits.medium: very low precipitation and temperature rising toward 30.0 C

drought.title: Drought

flood.explanation: Flood risk is 0.0% (low) because the forecast shows 0.0 mm of precipitation in the next 24 hours, then adjusts that signal using recent wetness versus same-season historical rainfall and terrain. The high-risk reference threshold is 50.0 mm/24h; values below 20.0 mm/24h are normally treated as low risk.

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.2 C. The forecast maximum for the next 24 hours is 13.6 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.high: near/above 38.0 C
normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 26.0% (low) because maximum forecast gusts are 10.0 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

storm.title: Storm / strong wind

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 13.1% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.2 C, and wind gusts 10.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score. Current temperature is 13.4 C.

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 13.1% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.2 C, and wind gusts 10.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score. Current temperature is 13.4 C.

normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices

normal_limits.high: above 35.0 mm/24h, critical reference at 50.0 mm/24h
normal_limits.low: below 20.0 mm/24h

flood.title: Flood

heatwave.explanation: Heatwave risk is 0.0% (low) because the observed maximum temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.8 C. Risk starts increasing above 30 C, and the high-risk reference threshold is 38.0 C.

normal_limits.high: near/above 38.0 C
normal_limits.low: below 30 C
normal_limits.medium: approximately 30-35.0 C

heatwave.title: Heatwave

storm.explanation: Storm risk is 36.4% (low) because maximum forecast gusts are 14.0 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

normal_limits.high: gusts above 17.5 m/s, critical reference 25.0 m/s, or very high CAPE
normal_limits.low: gusts below 10.0 m/s and low CAPE
normal_limits.medium: gusts between 10.0 and 17.5 m/s or moderate CAPE

storm.title: Storm / strong wind

wildfire.explanation: Spontaneous wildfire risk index is 8.9% (low). This is not a probability; it is a normalized warning index. The model now requires dry fuel conditions plus ignition weather, then uses wind mainly as a spread amplifier. It combines historical dryness, observed 24-hour maximum temperature 14.8 C, and wind gusts 14.0 m/s. Risk rises sharply above 32.0 C and above 12.0 m/s. Historical dryness is compared with previous years for the same season, so wind alone or one dry forecast day should not produce an excessive spontaneous-fire score.

normal_limits.high: temperature above 32.0 C, wind above 12.0 m/s, and dry soil
normal_limits.low: temperatures below 25 C, weak wind, and moist soil
normal_limits.medium: rising temperature, wind near 12.0 m/s, or drought signs

wildfire.title: Wildfire

drought.explanation: Drought risk is 30.0% (low) because expected precipitation is 2.0 mm/24h, observed mean temperature over the last 24 hours is 14.8 C, and soil moisture is unknown. For drought, precipitation below 5.0 mm/24h and temperatures above 30.0 C increase risk. The score now uses previous years for the same seasonal window, so one dry forecast day is not enough by itself to create high drought risk. In this calculation: precipitation moderately increases risk, temperature reduces risk, 30-day historical dryness reduces risk.

normal_limits.high: near-zero precipitation, dry soil, and temperature above 30.0 C
normal_limits.low: above 5.0 mm/24h or sufficiently moist soil
normal_limits.medium: very low precipitation and temperature rising toward 30.0 C

predictions.model_note: Forward predictions are warning-index forecasts, not probabilities. They use Open-Meteo daily forecast values combined with the same historical baseline and terrain context used by the current report.

resources.explanation: Local resource cache adds hydrology, water bodies, urban/impervious proxies, vegetation/forest, and agriculture context. These features refine relative zone exposure; they are not official hazard maps.

zone_analysis.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.
zone_analysis.method: Named Iasi AOI sectors using elevation samples, OSM hydrology/land-cover context, recent weather, historical baseline factors, and global AOI risk indices
